title: Separate topical edges from strict dependency edges
problem: The current schema allows belongs_to_topic, and the 16-paper graph contains four such edges. These edges are valid today but are weaker than concept dependencies and can blur the dependency graph the project is trying to expose.
proposed_change: Decide whether belongs_to_topic should remain in the core dependency graph. If kept, mark it as non-dependency metadata in audit/site output; otherwise remove it from prompts/schema and migrate existing topical edges.
acceptance_criteria: The relation taxonomy document or schema distinguishes strict dependency predicates from topical metadata; visualization can hide or de-emphasize topical edges; validation catches unintended topical edges if the relation is removed.

observations.context: Final graph has 4 belongs_to_topic edges out of 740. They came from mr4258055 and mr4574664 and passed validation because the relation is allowed in schema/prompt/validate.py. They are low-volume but semantically weaker than dependency predicates for the intended concept dependency graph.
